node.title: 💭 How Do You Effectively Use Pen and Paper While Solving Problems?

article.title: 💭 How Do You Effectively Use Pen and Paper While Solving Problems?
article.content:
Hey everyone 👋

I’ve noticed that many people recommend solving LeetCode problems using pen and paper, but I’m honestly not sure how to use it efficiently. Most of the problems I’ve solved so far have been entirely on-screen, and whenever I try to brainstorm on paper, I often get stuck or lose track of the question altogether.

I’d love to know:

How do you personally use pen and paper when solving coding problems?

What kind of notes do you make (e.g., examples, dry runs, patterns, or flow diagrams)?

How do you make the process efficient instead of slowing yourself down?

Any tips, examples, or photos of how you structure your thought process would be super helpful! 🙏

Thanks in advance for sharing your methods — I’m really trying to build better problem-solving habits.

PS: Adding tags to get better reach. Please forgive me for this.

post.content: i like to make a separate column on the page on the side for restrains, certain key points that i got from the test cases mentioned, what approach  i wanna use to solve it. i usually make a rough flow chart sort of thing first for the brute force, then i go on to try to optimize it.

I\u2019m using pen and paper for most algorithm problems by working from the test cases toward the expected result \u2014 it\u2019s kind of like doing math. I also look for patterns in those rewritten cases; for example, after writing down two or three (or more) cases, I try to identify any patterns and/or exceptions and note them down, either as a mathematical equation or a short sentence. Most of the time, things become clear at that point \u2014 if not, I go through more test cases.\n\nI also use GeoGebra Classic for math, geometry, and some more hard problems. It\u2019s not exactly pen and paper, but it helps me handle those kinds of problems more efficiently.
how effectively do you use it, ChatGPT?
